description: A realistic production incident queue with 15 bugs. Some CRITICAL alerts are noise from flaky tests. Some LOW-severity bugs are silently corrupting data. A cascade exists — fixing a symptom without fixing the root cause wastes budget. You cannot investigate everything. Choose wisely.
goal: Identify real issues vs noise. Some CRITICAL bugs are known flaky tests — harmless. Some LOW bugs are silently corrupting data. investigate() reveals the truth. Budget: 12 action points. investigate() costs 1, fix() costs 2.

bugs.error_message: HIGH: checkout-service intermittent 502 Bad Gateway on /api/v2/checkout/submit — upstream connection reset

bugs.error_message: HIGH: payment-service charge confirmations failing — downstream timeout from checkout-service
bugs.stack_trace:
goroutine 1203 [running]:
github.com/sregym/payment-service/internal/charge.(*ChargeConfirmer).Confirm(0xc000538000, {0xc0007a2100, 0x24})
	/app/internal/charge/confirmer.go:87 +0x2f5
github.com/sregym/payment-service/internal/handler.(*ChargeHandler).HandleConfirmation(0xc000492000, {0x1a4b5c0, 0xc0005b0000}, 0xc0007a2000)
	/app/internal/handler/charge_handler.go:63 +0x1a8

Error: context deadline exceeded after 10s waiting for checkout-service /api/v2/checkout/status
This failure is a DOWNSTREAM SYMPTOM. The payment-service charge confirmation calls checkout-service, which is returning 502s due to the upstream pod OOM issue (see BUG_M001). Fixing this bug alone will not resolve the issue — the root cause is the memory leak in checkout-service's upstream dependency.

See: https://wiki.internal/runbooks/search-service#ci-false-positive"}],"status":503}}

Note: products_v3 is NOT the production index. Production uses products_v7. This alert has fired 312 times in the last 30 days, always during deploy windows, and auto-resolves within 90 seconds.

bugs.error_message: CRITICAL: payment-service Stripe webhook signature verification FAILED — potential security breach
bugs.stack_trace:
goroutine 45 [running]:
github.com/sregym/payment-service/internal/webhook.(*StripeWebhookHandler).VerifySignature(0xc000234000, {0xc000456100, 0x120}, {0xc000456300, 0x40})
	/app/internal/webhook/stripe_handler.go:56 +0x1b2

Signature verification failed for endpoint /webhooks/stripe/v2-canary
Expected signing secret: whsec_test_***
Received header: Stripe-Signature: t=1712300000,v1=abc123...

*** THIS IS A KNOWN FALSE POSITIVE ***
The /v2-canary endpoint receives test webhook events from Stripe's sandbox environment during scheduled integration tests (runs every 15 minutes). The test signing secret rotates daily but the canary config updates hourly, causing a window of signature mismatches. These are TEST events, not production traffic. See: JIRA-4521 (wontfix). Production webhook endpoint /webhooks/stripe/v2 is unaffected.

bugs.error_message: LOW: payment-service reconciliation job minor discrepancy — 0.02% mismatch rate in daily settlement
bugs.stack_trace:
goroutine 12 [running]:
github.com/sregym/payment-service/internal/reconciliation.(*Reconciler).ReconcileBatch(0xc000298000, 0xc000634000)
	/app/internal/reconciliation/reconciler.go:178 +0x3f2

DATA CORRUPTION DETECTED:
  Settlement batch: 2024-04-06_daily
  Total transactions processed: 14,832
  Mismatched records: 3 (0.02%)
  Discrepancy details:
    - txn_id=ch_3Ox7bK2eZvKYlo: charged $149.99, settled $14.99 (decimal point shift)
    - txn_id=ch_3Ox8aM4fYwLZmp: charged $89.50, settled $895.00 (decimal point shift)
    - txn_id=ch_3Ox9cN6gZxMAnq: charged $250.00, settled $25.00 (decimal point shift)
  Pattern: All mismatches involve the new currency conversion path introduced in PR #4721 (merged 2024-04-03)
  Impact: $1,050.50 in incorrect settlements. SILENT DATA CORRUPTION — no user-facing errors, only caught by daily reconciliation batch.
  Currency conversion function: convertCents() truncating instead of rounding at line reconciler.go:134

bugs.error_message: MEDIUM: checkout-service cart persistence intermittent — carts lost on pod reschedule
bugs.stack_trace:
goroutine 334 [running]:
github.com/sregym/checkout-service/internal/cart.(*CartStore).Load(0xc000312000, {0xc000678100, 0x24})
	/app/internal/cart/store.go:92 +0x285

Error: cart data not found for session_id=sess_a8f3c2e1b4d7
  Storage backend: in-memory (not persisted to Redis)
  Pod: checkout-service-7f8b9c-xk2lp restarted 4 minutes ago (OOMKilled)
  Carts created since last restart: 12
  Carts lost on restart: 30
  Configuration issue: CART_STORAGE_BACKEND env var set to 'memory' instead of 'redis'
  This was changed in hotfix PR #3892 to work around a Redis serialization bug and never reverted.
  Impact: Users lose cart contents when pods reschedule, causing checkout abandonment.

bugs.error_message: HIGH: auth-service brute force detection triggered — 15,000 failed login attempts from single IP range
bugs.stack_trace:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/auth_service/security/brute_force_detector.py", line 56, in check_rate_limit
    if self.counter.get(ip_range) > self.threshold:
  File "/app/auth_service/security/brute_force_detector.py", line 62, in check_rate_limit
    raise BruteForceDetected(f"Rate limit exceeded for {ip_range}")
auth_service.security.BruteForceDetected: Rate limit exceeded for 10.0.0.0/8

*** KNOWN FALSE POSITIVE ***
Source IP range 10.0.0.0/8 is the internal Kubernetes pod network. These 'failed login attempts' are health check probes from the ingress controller that hit /api/v1/auth/login with empty credentials. The brute force detector does not exclude internal IPs. This alert fires every 6 hours when the sliding window counter resets. See: incident postmortem INC-2024-0287 (marked as wontfix, alert tuning backlogged).

bugs.error_message: MEDIUM: recommendation-service A/B test control group receiving treatment — experiment contamination
bugs.stack_trace:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/recommendation_service/experiments/ab_router.py", line 67, in route_request
    variant = self.assignment_store.get_variant(user_id, experiment_id)
  File "/app/recommendation_service/experiments/assignment_store.py", line 43, in get_variant
    cached = self.redis.get(f"exp:{experiment_id}:user:{user_id}")
  File "/app/recommendation_service/experiments/assignment_store.py", line 51, in get_variant
    # Cache miss — reassign
    return self._assign_new(user_id, experiment_id)

Experiment contamination detected:
  Experiment: rec_ranking_v3_test (started 2024-04-01)
  Expected control group size: 5000 users
  Actual control group size: 2847 users (43% leakage)
  Cause: Redis cache TTL set to 1h instead of experiment duration (30d).
  When cache expires, users are re-randomized and may switch groups.
  Impact: Experiment results invalid. 6 days of A/B data compromised.

bugs.error_message: LOW: user-data-service GDPR export job slow — average export time 45 minutes (SLO: 10 minutes)
bugs.stack_trace:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/user_data_service/gdpr/exporter.py", line 89, in export_user_data
    profile = await self.db.fetch_user_profile(user_id)
  File "/app/user_data_service/gdpr/exporter.py", line 112, in export_user_data
    activities = await self.db.fetch_activity_log(user_id, since=None)

DATA CORRUPTION DETECTED:
  During GDPR export for user_id=usr_8f2a1c3d, discovered data integrity violation:
  - users_v2.email: 'jane.doe@example.com'
  - users_v2.email_normalized: 'john.smith@company.org' (MISMATCH)
  - users_v2.phone_hash: references deleted record in phone_verification table
  - users_v2.address_id: FK points to address belonging to user_id=usr_7e3b2d4c (WRONG USER)
  
  Affected records found so far: 847 users with cross-linked address_id foreign keys
  Root cause: Migration script migrate_v1_to_v2.py (ran 2024-03-28) had a race condition in batch UPDATE that shuffled address_id values when concurrent writes occurred.
  Impact: SILENT DATA CORRUPTION — 847 users have incorrect addresses on file. No user-facing error. Compliance risk: GDPR exports contain wrong personal data.

bugs.error_message: MEDIUM: checkout-service promo code validation returning false positives — expired codes accepted
bugs.stack_trace:
goroutine 567 [running]:
github.com/sregym/checkout-service/internal/promo.(*PromoValidator).Validate(0xc000412000, {0xc000789100, 0x10})
	/app/internal/promo/validator.go:78 +0x1d3

Validation anomaly:
  Promo code: SPRING24-50OFF (expired 2024-04-01)
  Validation result: VALID (should be EXPIRED)
  Discount applied: 50% off order total
  
  Root cause: PromoValidator.Validate() compares expiry date using
    time.Now().Format("2006-01-02") < promo.ExpiryDate
  which performs string comparison, not date comparison.
  "2024-04-07" < "2024-04-01" is false (correct), BUT
  "2024-04-07" < "2024-1-01" evaluates incorrectly due to missing zero-padding in legacy promo records.
  
  Revenue impact: $3,420 in unauthorized discounts over 6 days.
  Affected orders: 22

bugs.error_message: HIGH: user-data-service profile update endpoint returning 500 for users with unicode names
bugs.stack_trace:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/user_data_service/api/profile.py", line 56, in update_profile
    validated = self.schema.validate(payload)
  File "/app/user_data_service/api/profile.py", line 72, in update_profile
    self.db.update_user(user_id, validated)
  File "/app/user_data_service/db/user_repo.py", line 98, in update_user
    await self.pool.execute(
      "UPDATE users_v2 SET display_name=$1, bio=$2 WHERE id=$3",
      data['display_name'], data['bio'], user_id
    )
asyncpg.exceptions.DataError: invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8": 0x80

Context:
  Failing input: display_name contains emoji + CJK characters (e.g., '田中太郎 🎮')
  Postgres encoding: UTF8 (correct)
  Issue: Profile update handler calls .encode('ascii', errors='replace') before DB write
    at profile.py:64 — this was added as a 'sanitization' step in PR #4102
  Impact: 85 users with non-ASCII display names cannot update their profiles.
  Workaround: None. Users are blocked from all profile edits.

  Throughput: 120 docs/sec (normal: 2,000 docs/sec)
  ES cluster health: GREEN
